questions.question_text: What multiplier increases a quantity by $8\%$?

options.why: Converted $8\%$ to $0.8$ instead of $0.08$, misplacing the decimal point.
Subtracted, giving the decrease multiplier instead of the increase multiplier.

questions.question_text: What multiplier decreases a quantity by $9\%$?

options.why: Added instead of subtracting, giving an increase multiplier.
Multiplied by $0.2$ instead of $0.8$, finding the amount removed.
Used the increase multiplier $1.2$ instead of the decrease multiplier $0.8$.

questions.question_text: A quantity is multiplied by $0.94$. What percentage change does this represent?

options.why: Read the multiplier $0.94$ itself as the size of the change.
Correct size but wrong direction; a multiplier below $1$ is a decrease.

questions.question_text: What multiplier increases a quantity by $120\%$?

options.why: Forgot to add the original $100\%$, using only the $120\%$ as $1.2$.
Converted $120\%$ to $0.12$ and added it to $1$.
